scope: inventory async/scheduling hard-negative requirements before #657 fixture expansion; no product behavior or exact admission changes

provenance.kind: hand-curated inventory from checked audit artifacts
provenance.row_key_note: audited_surfaces lists unique surface IDs; surface_row_keys_by_language preserves every counted primary lifecycle audit row including repeated surface IDs with different operations
provenance.supplemental_priced_note: supplemental_priced_surfaces records Promise continuation/rejection reporting rows and timer/scheduler priced rows that are already present in checked artifacts but are not part of the primary scheduling-lifecycle audit's 82 counted rows

evidence_quantification_policy.positive_fixture_groups: Runnable test files or test symbols that contain admitted or positive semantic-kernel evidence. Reporting JSON artifacts are not counted as positive fixtures.
evidence_quantification_policy.hard_negative_fixture_groups: Runnable test files or test symbols that assert unsafe semantic convergence stays split. A mixed positive/negative test file may appear in both fixture arrays only when it contains both kinds of assertions.
evidence_quantification_policy.reporting_artifact_evidence_groups: Checked JSON artifacts and audit reports that price or classify evidence. These are counted separately from runnable fixture groups.
evidence_quantification_policy.counting_unit: Each array entry is one evidence group, usually a test file, a test symbol, or a checked artifact path.

validation_commands: jq -e '([.matrix[].source_prevalence_occurrences] | add) == .quantitative_summary.source_prevalence_occurrences_scoped and ([.matrix[].surface_rows] | add) == .quantitative_summary.audited_surface_rows and ([.matrix[].existing_positive_fixture_evidence | length] | add) == .quantitative_summary.current_positive_fixture_groups and ([.matrix[].existing_hard_negative_evidence | length] | add) == .quantitative_summary.current_hard_negative_fixture_groups and ([.matrix[].existing_reporting_artifact_evidence | length] | add) == .quantitative_summary.current_reporting_artifact_evidence_groups and ([.matrix[].missing_hard_negative_classes | length] | add) == .quantitative_summary.missing_hard_negative_classes' bench/recall_loss/issue-655-hard-negative-matrix-2026-07-02.v1.json

jq -e '([.surface_row_keys_by_language[][] | select(test("reporting-supported"))] | length) == .quantitative_summary.reporting_supported_closed_boundary_surface_rows and ([.surface_row_keys_by_language[][] | select(test("closed-boundary") and (test("reporting-supported") | not))] | length) == .quantitative_summary.closed_boundary_surface_rows and ([.surface_row_keys_by_language[][] | select(test("exact-supported"))] | length) == .quantitative_summary.exact_supported_boundary_surface_rows and ([.surface_row_keys_by_language[][] | select(test("superseded"))] | length) == .quantitative_summary.superseded_overlap_boundary_surface_rows' bench/recall_loss/issue-655-hard-negative-matrix-2026-07-02.v1.json

bash -lc 'mkdir -p target && jq -r '\''.surfaces[] | select(.language != "c") | (.surface + " :: " + .operation + " :: " + .status + " :: occurrences=" + (.occurrences|tostring) + " :: repos=" + (.repos|tostring))'\'' bench/recall_loss/scheduling-lifecycle-boundary-audit-java-completablefuture-receiver-split-2026-07-02.v1.json | sort > target/issue-655-primary.rows && jq -r '\''.surface_row_keys_by_language[][]'\'' bench/recall_loss/issue-655-hard-negative-matrix-2026-07-02.v1.json | sort > target/issue-655-matrix.rows && diff -u target/issue-655-primary.rows target/issue-655-matrix.rows'

jq -e 'def sum_occ($re): [.surface_row_keys_by_language[][] | select(test($re)) | capture("occurrences=(?<n>[0-9]+)").n | tonumber] | add; sum_occ("reporting-supported") == .quantitative_summary.reporting_supported_closed_boundary_occurrences and sum_occ("closed-boundary") == (.quantitative_summary.reporting_supported_closed_boundary_occurrences + .quantitative_summary.closed_boundary_occurrences) and sum_occ("exact-supported") == .quantitative_summary.exact_supported_boundary_occurrences and sum_occ("superseded") == .quantitative_summary.superseded_overlap_boundary_occurrences' bench/recall_loss/issue-655-hard-negative-matrix-2026-07-02.v1.json

jq -e '([.matrix[].supplemental_priced_surfaces[]?] | length) == .quantitative_summary.supplemental_priced_surface_rows and ([.matrix[].supplemental_priced_surfaces[]? | select(.category == "promise-continuation")] | length) == .quantitative_summary.supplemental_js_ts_promise_continuation_reporting_surface_rows and (([.matrix[].supplemental_priced_surfaces[]? | select(.category == "promise-continuation") | .occurrences] | add) // 0) == .quantitative_summary.supplemental_js_ts_promise_continuation_occurrences and ([.matrix[].supplemental_priced_surfaces[]? | select(.category == "timer-scheduler")] | length) == .quantitative_summary.supplemental_js_ts_timer_scheduler_priced_surface_rows and (([.matrix[].supplemental_priced_surfaces[]? | select(.category == "timer-scheduler") | .occurrences] | add) // 0) == .quantitative_summary.supplemental_js_ts_timer_scheduler_occurrences' bench/recall_loss/issue-655-hard-negative-matrix-2026-07-02.v1.json

python3 -c "import json,pathlib; d=json.load(open('bench/recall_loss/issue-655-hard-negative-matrix-2026-07-02.v1.json')); refs=d['source_artifacts']+[i.split('::',1)[0] for r in d['matrix'] for k in ('existing_positive_fixture_evidence','existing_hard_negative_evidence','existing_reporting_artifact_evidence') for i in r.get(k, []) if i.startswith(('crates/','bench/'))]; missing=[p for p in refs if not pathlib.Path(p).exists()]; assert not missing, missing"

missing_hard_negative_classes: Promise.then receiver, fulfillment, rejection, and callback-obligation permutations in one local suite
Promise.catch rejection continuation and callback demand/effect separation
Promise.finally settlement preservation versus rejection override and callback demand/effect separation
Promise.reject rejected-value channel versus fulfilled and synchronous value channels
Promise aggregate dynamic-iterable lifecycle versus literal aggregate recovery
Promise aggregate all-fulfilled versus first-settled and all-settled channel distinctions
new Promise executor timing, synchronous throw-to-rejection, and multi-settlement ordering
AbortSignal cancellation/liveness separated from fulfillment and rejection recovery
setTimeout and setImmediate one-shot timer scheduling versus synchronous callback invocation
setInterval repeated-emission cardinality and liveness versus one-shot timers
clearTimeout, clearInterval, and cancelAnimationFrame cancellation lifecycle separation
queueMicrotask microtask ordering versus direct callback invocation and Promise continuation ordering
requestAnimationFrame frame scheduling and cancellation versus timers and microtasks
scheduler.yield and scheduler.wait ordering, timing, and cancellation/liveness versus Promise.resolve and global timers

recommended_fixture_order.reason: The latest audits leave the highest-risk closed JS/TS fixture queue: setTimeout 780, new Promise 795, Promise.all 397, AbortSignal 260, clearTimeout 133, setInterval 73, setImmediate 57, requestAnimationFrame 43, and Promise.race 32 occurrences.
Go has 31,500 reporting-supported protocol occurrences, high concurrency risk, and compact language-level surfaces.
Swift has 37,758 reporting-supported occurrences plus a 26,608-occurrence superseded exception row; task and exception-channel hard negatives should be locked before exact work.
Rust has 13,228 reporting-supported async/runtime occurrences and existing runtime-provenance tests that can be extended without new public API.
Java has 1,269 Future/Executor reporting-supported occurrences, 500 exact-supported stream adapter occurrences, and a 1,496-occurrence Stream lifecycle residual that needs lifecycle hard negatives.
Python has 6,439 reporting-supported async/generator occurrences and no remaining closed rows in this audit, so fixture work mostly hardens already named boundaries.
Ruby has 4,873 reporting-supported runtime/exception/yield occurrences and should follow once the shared scheduling and exception-channel fixture idioms are established.

next_policy: #657 should add focused hard-negative fixture suites in this order before any behavior-changing exact admission under #653. #654, #656, and #658 should link this matrix when auditing capability vocabulary, legacy labels, and oracle-exclusion accounting.
